steps.title: Spot the contrast: impossible vs. eventually solved
steps.content: The key signal is the contradiction between “though it seems to many mathematicians” and “eventually yielded.” The conjecture appeared unsolvable to mathematicians, yet it was solved. The blank describes that apparent impossibility.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the contrast
steps.content:
Insuperable means “impossible to overcome.” The sentence captures a contrast: the conjecture seemed insurmountable to many mathematicians, yet it eventually yielded — it was solved.

Unequivocal (clear), ineluctable (inevitable), and irreproachable (faultless) don't address this sense of overwhelming difficulty.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
